transition_goal: Production-grade local Rust parity with explicit diagnostic blockers for upstream contracts that are not present in this snapshot.

summary.current_position: Core local CLI, agent loop, tools, sessioning, structured output, and supported MCP transports are usable; full product parity still depends on plugin, bundled-skill, remote-session, and service contracts.
recommended_first_slice: Freeze a live parity matrix and keep it test-backed.
Close in-repo skill, hook, and plugin gaps where contracts exist.
Add quotas, sandbox profiles, guardrails, redaction, and audit exports before expanding risky automation.
Modularize the CLI/TUI before adding richer editor and dashboard surfaces.

parity_items.name: core-runtime
parity_items.current: Rust conversation runtime supports streaming assistant turns, tool calls, sessions, permissions, and usage tracking.

parity_items.name: tool-registry
parity_items.current: Native Rust tools, TypeScript-name aliases, schemas, dynamic MCP tools, and dedicated renderers are present for the practical local surface.
needed: Keep diagnostic-only tools honest until a real runtime exists.
Continue adding output schemas/renderers as new tools land.

parity_items.name: mcp-transports
parity_items.current: stdio, direct HTTP/SSE, and websocket MCP execution are supported; sdk, simcoe-ai-proxy, and headersHelper paths return structured unsupported-runtime diagnostics.
needed: Import or document the upstream SDK adapter contract.
Import or document the simcoe-ai-proxy websocket/session adapter contract.
Define headersHelper output and credential-injection behavior.

parity_items.name: plugins
parity_items.current: The CLI can inspect archived plugin command/module surfaces, but there is no live plugin loader, lifecycle, marketplace flow, or extension registration path.
needed: Define plugin manifest and lifecycle schema.
Load enabled plugins from disk.
Register plugin-provided commands, hooks, tools, skills, and MCP servers.
Implement reload, enable, disable, install, and update flows.

parity_items.name: skills
parity_items.current: Local markdown skills are usable and archived bundled skills are inspectable, but bundled and MCP-built skill execution remains absent.
needed: Load bundled skill packages as executable prompt/runtime assets.
Add skill reload/change detection.
Port or replace MCP skill-builder behavior.
Integrate skill use with session/team memory where available.

parity_items.name: hooks
parity_items.current: PreToolUse and PostToolUse shell hooks are parsed, inspected, and executed, including deny/warn behavior.
needed: Add broader hook event families where needed.
Define a safe mutation/rewrite pipeline.
Add hook management commands beyond config inspection.

parity_items.name: remote-transport
parity_items.current: Rust reports bootstrap/proxy readiness and structured local turn transport metadata, but it does not run the TypeScript live remote websocket/session path.
needed: Obtain the websocket/session protocol contract.
Port live remote structured I/O with resumable state and tests.
Keep local api-stream metadata explicit while remote is unavailable.

parity_items.name: service-ecosystem
parity_items.current: Core auth/API/MCP/usage foundations exist, but analytics, settings sync, team memory sync, notifier, voice, and policy-limit services are not ported.
needed: Inventory service contracts that are actually required for this product.
Port only services with clear local or remote contracts.
Keep unavailable service families documented as out-of-scope or blocked.

industry_benchmarks.gap: The product is terminal-first and does not yet expose editor-native diagnostics, inline edits, symbol navigation, code actions, or session tracking.
needed: Add a language-server or VS Code extension bridge.
Surface diffs, diagnostics, chat, and agent session progress inside the editor.
Replace LSPTool diagnostics with real editor-attached execution when available.

industry_benchmarks.benchmark: Cursor codebase understanding and Copilot repository memory
industry_benchmarks.gap: Retrieval/context scripts exist, but there is no incremental symbol graph, reference graph, impact map, or test ownership index for the Rust CLI.
needed: Build file, symbol, reference, dependency, and test ownership indexes.
Use the index for planning, tool choice, and post-edit verification.
Persist repository-scoped facts with invalidation.

industry_benchmarks.benchmark: OpenAI Agents guardrails and hosted/local sandbox patterns
industry_benchmarks.gap: Permission modes and allowlists exist, but quotas, network/file sandbox profiles, redaction, and human-review checkpoints are not complete.
needed: Add per-tool/session quotas, rate limits, and timeouts.
Add sandbox profiles for shell, file, and network tools.
Block or require approval for destructive commands, credential access, exfiltration, and mass edits.
Add secret redaction and audit export.

industry_benchmarks.benchmark: GitHub Copilot cloud agent and Claude Code remote/continuous workflows
industry_benchmarks.gap: Local task manifests and session resume exist, but durable cloud/background sessions with progress tracking, notifications, cancellation, and PR artifacts are not complete.
needed: Add durable background run state and progress logs.
Expose cancellation, artifact collection, and notifications.
Connect runs to GitHub issues, PRs, and review comments.

industry_benchmarks.benchmark: MCP clients across Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, VS Code, and GitHub workflows
industry_benchmarks.gap: Supported transports are robust, but blocked transports and prompt/resource conformance need explicit profiles and tests.
needed: Add conformance fixtures for tools, resources, prompts, auth, and dynamic allowlisting.
Publish compatibility profiles for common servers and client expectations.
Keep unsupported transports categorized with remediation hints.

industry_benchmarks.benchmark: OpenAI tracing/evals and enterprise release gates
industry_benchmarks.gap: Root eval scripts exist, but Rust CLI agent workflows lack unified trace spans, benchmark scenarios, and release scorecards.
needed: Trace model calls, tool calls, MCP calls, hooks, retries, approvals, and errors.
Add code-edit, bug-fix, refactor, PR-review, MCP, and security benchmarks.
Wire scorecards into CI release gates.

industry_benchmarks.benchmark: Claude Code terminal UX and Cursor visual review
industry_benchmarks.gap: The CLI works, but the active REPL remains monolithic and lacks a persistent HUD, collapsible tool output, rich diffs, pager, and editor-style review panels.
needed: Modularize LiveCli out of main.rs.
Add status bar, live token/cost HUD, colored diffs, pager, and collapsible tool output.
Make full-screen TUI or web dashboard optional after module boundaries are stable.

blocked_contracts.name: mcp-sdk-transport
blocked_contracts.needed_contract: Upstream SDK adapter execution contract and request/response lifecycle.
blocked_contracts.current_behavior: Rust reports unsupported-runtime before attempting execution.

blocked_contracts.name: simcoe-ai-proxy-transport
blocked_contracts.needed_contract: Websocket/session adapter equivalent to remote/SessionsWebSocket.ts and remote/sdkMessageAdapter.ts.
blocked_contracts.current_behavior: Rust reports remote/proxy readiness diagnostics and blocks live execution.

blocked_contracts.name: remote-headers-helper
blocked_contracts.needed_contract: headersHelper invocation, output schema, credential scope, refresh behavior, and failure semantics.
blocked_contracts.current_behavior: Rust leaves headersHelper-backed MCP servers inspection-only.

blocked_contracts.name: plugin-runtime
blocked_contracts.needed_contract: Plugin manifest, lifecycle, extension contribution points, and security boundaries.
blocked_contracts.current_behavior: Rust exposes archived plugin inspection only.

blocked_contracts.name: bundled-and-mcp-skills
blocked_contracts.needed_contract: Bundled skill execution environment and MCP skill-builder behavior.
blocked_contracts.current_behavior: Rust runs local markdown skills and inspects archived bundled skill samples.
